title: Live venue reference (capacity, costs, real mappings)

venue_rank_indexing.venue_rank_index: Integer **0–6** in the same order: **0 = S** (largest / most expensive class here) through **6 = F** (smallest room). This is a **venue-size** axis; it reuses letters from `database/idol_group_rank_table.md` but measures **room scale**, not group commercial tier.

assumptions.capacity: Standing (or max published) capacities are **approximate** from press, hall sites, and Wikipedia-ja summaries; seated-only shows differ. For **fixed-seat concert halls**, `capacity_standing_about` often uses the **published seat count** as a size proxy (not a standing-room figure).
assumptions.venue_rank_ef_boundary: **F** = `livehouse_micro` (about **50–180** standing). **E** = `livehouse_small` (**181–300**) so rank-D **~300 cap** one-mans sit in **E**, not **F**.
assumptions.stadium_design_band: **Venue rank S / `stadium` scale** uses capacity band **25,000–60,000** for sim sizing (rent curve anchor). Real venues with **>60k** official capacity stay listed with `above_stadium_design_band_max: true` (e.g. Nissan Stadium, Japan National Stadium fixed seat count ~68k).
assumptions.hall_large_max_model: **Venue rank B / `hall_large`** is **3,101–7,000** model concert capacity (see `venue_scales`). **Makuhari Event Hall** ([Makuhari Messe facility guide](https://www.m-messe.co.jp/en/organizers/guide/eventhall/guide_eventhall)) is the reference **largest hall_large**; the operator cites **~9,000** for large-scale layouts—`capacity_standing_about` uses **7,000** to match the tier ceiling unless you fork the JSON.
assumptions.arena_band: **Venue rank A / `arena`** is **7,001–24,999** (everything above `hall_large` max and below the stadium band).
assumptions.club_gap_fill: `livehouse_club` (**D**) extends to **799** so **651–799** is covered between the old club ceiling and **C** at **800**.
assumptions.venue_rent: **Ballpark** single-day hall rental + house tech buyout for an indie/idol-sized promoter block—not audited venue quotes.
assumptions.live_ops_cost: Day-of ops **excluding** monthly salaries: local staff, basic PA, security share. Default formula matches `FinanceSystem.build_daily_breakdown` per live: `18_000 + member_count × 4_500` JPY (see `database/finance/finance_system.py`). Scales below add a **venue-size multiplier** on top of that base for larger rooms.
assumptions.event_fee_note: `FinanceSystem.estimate_venue_fee` supports **weekday vs weekend/holiday** and booking plans (`half_day_a`, `half_day_b`, `full_day`) for capacity ≤ `small_venue_capacity_threshold` (300), anchored to TwinBox chart points (150/200 cap) and interpolated by capacity. Legacy `small_venue_event_fee_yen` remains as compatibility fallback.
assumptions.type_1_ticket_mix_venue_sa: For **venue ranks S (`stadium`) and A (`arena`)**, **live type 1** (premium concert) **house** inventory is modeled as **70%** at **full-view** list price and **10%** at **obstructed / partial-view** price; the **remaining ~20%** is not forced to one tier (VIP, fanclub, comps, promoter holds, etc.). Yen anchors match `database/idol_group_rank_table.md` (**¥10,000** full, **¥8,000** obstructed). See `type_1_premium_concert_ticket_inventory`.
assumptions.rank_b_ticket_mix_hall_large: **Venue rank B (`hall_large`)**: **85%** of house at **full** list and **15%** at **partial** list (same yen anchors as type 1 for the partial slice unless you fork). Blended **¥9,700** per unit capacity from priced shares only (`0.85×10,000 + 0.15×8,000`). `capacity_standing_about` for **Makuhari Event Hall** stays **7,000** (tier ceiling); **~9,000** remains operator large-layout reference only.
assumptions.type_4_taiban_venue_preference: Type 4 taiban is usually hosted at **rank C / hall_medium**; **rank B / hall_large** and **rank D / livehouse_club** are secondary and less frequent options.

type_1_premium_concert_ticket_inventory.remainder_note: Other price tiers, allocations, or non-revenue holds—blend per event; not defaulted to a single anchor.
type_1_premium_concert_ticket_inventory.expected_ticket_revenue_formula: 0.7 * full_price_ticket_jpy + 0.1 * partial_or_obstructed_ticket_jpy; remainder share (0.2) treated as 0 in this scalar (see remainder_note).

rank_b_hall_large_ticket_inventory.price_anchor_source: Same anchors as type 1 full / partial in `database/idol_group_rank_table.md`; rank-B split is venue-tier policy in this file.
rank_b_hall_large_ticket_inventory.capacity_model_note: Reference largest B: Makuhari uses `capacity_standing_about` 7,000 with operator ~9,000 noted separately on the venue row.

venue_scales.label: Arena-class indoor venue (7,001+; large bowls below stadium band)
Hall (single balcony / flat floor; rank C: 800–3,100)

recorded_venues.name: Japan National Stadium
recorded_venues.capacity_notes: Wikipedia: fixed ~68,000 (athletics mode); expandable to ~80,000 with temporary seating over track for some events.

recorded_venues.name: Saitama Super Arena
recorded_venues.name_ja: さいたまスーパーアリーナ

recorded_venues.name: Tokyo Dome
recorded_venues.capacity_notes: Wikipedia: events about 42,000–55,000 depending on layout; baseball 45,600; maximum about 57,000. Value here is a mid concert-style estimate within the stadium design band.

recorded_venues.name: Ariake Arena
recorded_venues.capacity_notes: Wikipedia: up to 15,000 with temporary seating; 12,000 fixed seats.

recorded_venues.name: TwinBox AKIHABARA
recorded_venues.capacity_notes: Facility overview lists TwinBoxAKIHABARA (B1). Fee chart is on the same about page; visible text includes daytime slots and extension fee (JPY 15,000/hour).

recorded_venues.name: TwinBox GARAGE
recorded_venues.capacity_notes: Facility overview lists TwinBoxGARAGE (1F). Fee chart is on the same about page; visible text includes daytime slots and extension fee (JPY 15,000/hour).

type_4_taiban_venue_preference.note: Usually rank C; rank B or D used less often by demand, lineup mix, or promo intent.
